data.content:
I could not run the app on this machine because the iOS toolchain is incomplete. The active developer directory is /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools, not a full Xcode install, so both xcodebuild and simctl fail. I also searched the machine for Xcode and did not find an installed app bundle. The project itself is a Swift package app target in [StreamCaster/Package.swift](StreamCaster/Package.swift), and there are currently no XCTest targets or .xctestplan files in the workspace, so there is no automated test suite to run yet.

To get this running locally, install full Xcode first, then switch the developer directory:

sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch

After that, from /Users/alex/Code/rtmp-client/iphone, use either Xcode or the CLI. In Xcode, open [StreamCaster/Package.swift](StreamCaster/Package.swift), let SwiftPM resolve dependencies, pick an iPhone simulator, and run the StreamCaster scheme. From the CLI, the flow should be:

xcodebuild -list -package-path StreamCaster
xcrun simctl list devices available
xcodebuild -scheme StreamCaster -package-path StreamCaster -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16" build

If you want, I can do one of these next:
1. Add a minimal XCTest target so the project has runnable tests.
2. Help you create an Xcode project or workspace wrapper if you do not want to run it as a pure Swift package.
3. Re-run the build and simulator launch as soon as Xcode is installed.

data.content:
The official Xcode App Store page is open in the browser. On this Mac, the straightforward path is App Store install; the `mas` CLI is not installed, so I can’t trigger a one-command install.

Install steps:
1. In the App Store page for Xcode, click `Get` or `Install`.
2. Sign in with your Apple ID if prompted.
3. Wait for the download and install to finish. Xcode is large, so this can take a while.
4. Launch Xcode once and accept the license/components prompt.
5. Then run:

```bash
sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
sudo xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
```

You can verify the install with:

```bash
xcodebuild -version
xcrun simctl list devices available
```

Once that succeeds, I can immediately take over again and build/run the iOS app in the simulator.

Let me also check if there's a way to perform a build check in the meantime. One option is to temporarily add `macOS` as a supported platform in the Package.swift and try a macOS build. But this won't work because the code uses iOS-specific APIs like UIKit, AVFoundation (some iOS-only parts), etc.

Actually, many of the AVFoundation APIs (like AVCaptureSession, AVAudioSession) are available on macOS too. 

UIKit is the real blocker though, and SwiftUI behaves differently on macOS. Rather than trying to work around the platform differences, I should just wait for the download to finish and do a code review in the meantime to spot any obvious issues.

data.content:
Still at 8.4% — at roughly 3–4 MB/s the remaining ~7.7 GB will take about 35–40 minutes. Here's the current status and what happens when it finishes:

**What's been done:**
- Fixed the Package.swift dependency: `KSCrashRecording` → `"Recording"` (the correct SPM product name for KSCrash 2.5.1)
- All Swift packages resolved cleanly (HaishinKit 2.2.4, KSCrash 2.5.1)
- No compile errors detected by the IDE, no crash-prone stubs found

**Still in progress:**
- iOS 26.3.1 Simulator runtime downloading: 8.4% (703 MB of 8.39 GB)
  - Log: `tail -3 /tmp/ios-runtime-download.log`

**Once it finishes**, I'll run these steps automatically if you come back to this chat:
```bash
# 1. Create simulator
xcrun simctl create "StreamCaster-iPhone16" \
  "com.apple.CoreSimulator.SimDeviceType.iPhone-16" \
  "com.apple.CoreSimulator.SimRuntime.iOS-26-3"

# 2. Boot it
xcrun simctl boot <device-uuid>

# 3. Build + install + launch
xcodebuild \
  -workspace StreamCaster/.swiftpm/xcode/package.xcworkspace \
  -scheme StreamCaster \
  -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,name=StreamCaster-iPhone16" \
  -configuration Debug build

xcrun simctl install booted <path/to/StreamCaster.app>
xcrun simctl launch booted com.port80.app
```

Come back once the download bar says "100%" or you can re-invoke me — I'll pick it up from there and get the app launched.
